species.citation: Lam. , Encycl. (Lamarck) 1 (1783) 254. = Aristolochia tagala Cham. , Linnaea 7 (1832) 207 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 186; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 120; Ding Hou, Blumea 29 (1983) 232; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1984) 94. = Aristolochia mindanaensis Warb. , Fragm. Fl. Philipp. (1905) 169 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 119; Type: Warburg 14772. = Aristolochia subdigitata Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 283; Type: Untraced, illustrative specimen: Merrill Species Blancoanae 289 (US), Luzon: Between Manila and Pasig, Mar-1911.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. BASILAN, CATANDUANES, CEBU (photos), LEYTE, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Bataan, Cagayan, Camarines Sur, Cavite, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, La Union, Laguna, NCR, Pampanga, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Davao, Zamboanga Sibugay, Zamboanga del Norte (photos), Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, PALAWAN, POLILLO, SAMAR.
species.notes: Forests and thickets, low and medium elevation. 0-800(-1350)m.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 13c (1918) 9 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 119; Ding Hou, Blumea 29 (1983) 227; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1984) 91; Type: BS 30370 Ramos.
species.distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra, Taiwan. CATANDUANES, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Moist lowland forest along small streams. (?-)1500-2100m.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 13c (1918) 280 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 119; Ding Hou, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1984) 103; Type: BS 28480 Ramos & Edaño (US, iso), Luzon: Quezon, Mt Binuang. May-1917.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Quezon.
species.notes: Damp forests along streams at low elevation.

species.notes: Thickets and forests near or along streams at low elevation.

species.notes: Damp forest near river at low elevation. Only known from the type collection.

species.citation: Quisumb. , PJS 41 (1930) 322 ; Ding Hou, Blumea 27 (1981) 306; Blumea 29 (1983) 242; Fl. Males. Ser. 1, 10 (1984) 75; Naive et al., Nordic J. Bot. e04153 (2023) 2; Type: FB 30249 Roque (UC, holo), Mindanao: Lanao, Santo Cogon, dipterocarp forest, c. 150m, 22-Apr-1926.
species.distribution: Borneo, Philippines. MINDANAO: Lanao, Zamboanga Sibugay, Zamboanga del Norte (photos), Zamboanga del Sur (photos).

species.citation: (Blume) Ding Hou , Blumea 27 (1981) 328 ; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1984) 79; Naive et al., Nordic J. Bot. e04153 (2023) 4; ≡ Apama tomentosa (Blume) Engl. , Nat. Pflanzenfam. (Engler & Prantl) 3(1) (1888) 272; ≡ Ceramium tomentosum Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826-1827) 1135; Type: from Java. = Bragantia affinis Planch. ex Rolfe , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1913 (1913) 265 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 120; Type: Cuming 1689 (K, lecto), Panay; Merrill 6700 (BM, K, PNH, US, syn), Panay: Dumarao, Mar-1910; BS 13370 Ramos, Luzon: Tayabas, Tagcauayan, Mar-1911. = Bragantia brevipes Merr. , PJS 17 (1920) 248 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 120; Type: BS 34811 Ramos & Pascasio (BM, BO, K, US, iso), Surigao, Apr-1919.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, India, Java, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nicobar Isls, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. ALABAT, LEYTE (photos), LUZON: Quezon, MINDANAO: Surigao, Zamboanga del Sur, PANAY, SAMAR.
species.notes: Moist forests along small streams at low and medium elevation. J.V. LaFrankie (pers. comm. 27-Apr-2016) considered Philippine plants under the name Thottea tomentosa distinct at the species level. P.B. Pelser (10-May-2020): photos here by Tandang show a flower with eight stamens, but Thottea tomentosa is supposed to consistently have six. Because the protologues of Bragantia affinis and Bragantia brevipes also mention flowers with six stamens, this plant might represent a distinct species. Naive et al. (2023) appear to consider only plants from Zamboanga del Sur to belong to Thottea tomentosa. 150-250m.
